POS RE REPLY SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT WESTERN ATHLETIC CLUBS, INC., DBA SAN FRANCISCO TENNIS CLUB

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WESTERN ATHLETIC CLUBS, INCORPORATED DBA SF TENNIS CLUB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN TO SHOW THAT UNDISPUTED FACTS SUPPORT EACH ELEMENT OF THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. (ANDERSEN V. METALCLAD INSULATION CORP. (1992) 72 CAL.APP.4TH 284, 289.) WHILE THE DOCTRINE OF ASSUMPTION OF THE RISK PROVIDES THAT THERE IS NO DUTY TO ELIMINATE RISKS THAT ARE INHERENT IN A SPORT OR ACTIVITY, "UNNECESSARILY DANGEROUS EQUIPMENT IS NOT CONSIDERED AN INHERENT RISK OF A SPORT." (ZIPUSCH V. LA WORKOUT, INC. (2007) 155 CAL.APP.4TH 1281, 1292.) DEFENDANTS HAVE PROVIDED NO EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING HOW AN INDO BOARD IS PROPERLY USED, AND THEREFORE, HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THEY PROVIDED THE INDO BOARD FOR USE TO CLUB MEMBERS IN SUCH A WAY THAT ALLOWED FOR PROPER USAGE. AS SUCH, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE INDO BOARD WAS NOT "UNNECESSARILY DANGEROUS EQUIPMENT." EVEN ASSUMING THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR BURDEN IN ESTABLISHING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH BREACH OF ANY DUTY BECAUSE THERE WAS A LABEL ON THE INDO BOARD THAT WARNED CLUB MEMBERS TO USE THE BOARD AT THEIR OWN RISK (UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT, NO. 9.), PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THIS WARNING WAS SUFFICIENT, GIVEN THAT PLACING THE BOARD ON A HARD SURFACE, AS OPPOSED TO CARPET, INCREASED THE RISK OF INJURY. (ADDITIONAL DISPUTED FACT ("ADF"), NO. 34.) ADDITIONALLY, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD A DUTY TO FURNISH MORE INFORMATION TO CLUB MEMBERS USING THE BOARD FOR THE FIRST TIME. (ADF, NO. 32-33.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR # 9581

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MAUREEN A. HARRINGTON OF THE GREENFIELD/SULLIVAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 283-1776 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JENIFER J. CAPABIANCO OF THE SELMAN BREITMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 979-0400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WESTERN ATHLETIC CLUBS, INCORPORATED DBA SF TENNIS CLUB'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Defendants have not met their initial burden to show that undisputed facts support each element of the affirmative defense. (Andersen v. Metalclad Insulation Corp. (1992) 72 Cal.App.4th 284, 289.) While the doctrine of assumption of the risk provides that there is no duty to eliminate risks that are inherent in a sport or activity, "unnecessarily dangerous equipment is not considered an inherent risk of a sport." (Zipusch v. LA Workout, Inc. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1281, 1292.) Defendants have provided no evidence establishing how an Indo Board is properly used, and therefore, have not established that they provided the Indo Board for use to club members in such a way that allowed for proper usage. As such, Defendants have not established that the Indo Board was not "unnecessarily dangerous equipment." Even assuming that Defendants have met their burden in establishing that Plaintiff cannot establish breach of any duty because there was a label on the Indo Board that warned club members to use the Board at their own risk (Undisputed Material Fact, no. 9.), Plaintiff has provided evidence establishing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether this warning was sufficient, given that placing the Board on a hard surface, as opposed to carpet, increased the risk of injury. (Additional Disputed Fact ("ADF"), no. 34.) Additionally, there is a triable issue of material fact as whether Defendant had a duty to furnish more information to club members using the Board for the first time. (ADF, no. 32-33.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Angie Diner, CSR # 9581 =(302/PJB)
